title: Disabling logging is security-sensitive
description: Disabling logging on cloud resources prevents the detection and investigation of security incidents, operational issues, and compliance violations. Logging is essential for monitoring, auditing, and responding to suspicious activity.
message: Logging is disabled. Enable logging to ensure visibility and traceability.
impact: Without logging, unauthorized or malicious activity may go undetected, and it may be impossible to investigate incidents or prove compliance.
recommendation: Enable logging for all critical resources (e.g., S3, CloudTrail, GCP Audit Logs). Regularly review logs and integrate with SIEM or monitoring solutions.

noncompliant.description: S3 bucket is created without logging enabled.

compliant.description: S3 bucket is created with logging enabled.

devsecops.security: Logging is critical for detecting and investigating security incidents.
devsecops.automation: CI/CD pipelines should enforce logging on all critical resources.
devsecops.compliance: Supports compliance with audit and monitoring requirements (e.g., PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR).

why_is_this_an_issue: Without logging, you lose visibility into resource access and changes, making it difficult to detect, investigate, or respond to security incidents.
how_to_fix: Enable logging for all critical resources. Regularly review and analyze logs for suspicious activity.

logic.description: Ensure logging is enabled for storage resources.
